title: trifolium pamphylicum
intro: Trifolium pamphylicum, also known as Cyprus clover, is a perennial legume native to the eastern Mediterranean region, particularly in Cyprus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Trifolium pamphylicum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
